citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care.
citations.narrative:
On November 24, 2025, a technician discovered a deceased male rabbit (1M024) on a long-term surgical study. A second
male rabbit (1M017) was found in poor condition exhibiting a body condition score of 3/9, severe malocclusion, and oral
ulcers; it was euthanized. USDA reviewed records and found that staff failed to weigh the rabbits at the required
frequency, did not recognize weight loss, teeth abnormalities, and did not report these problems. A lack of timely and
accurate communication with the attending veterinarian delayed adequate treatment, resulting in illness and mortality.
Daily observation of all animals to assess their health and wellbeing must be done. Also, a mechanism of direct and
frequent communication is required so that timely and accurate information on problems of animal health, behavior, and
well-being is conveyed to the attending veterinarian. Corrected before time of inspection on March 24, 2026.

Four male beagles (4267673, 4527772, 4529422, and 4661657) did not have access to water from January 27, 2025, to
February 2, 2025, despite husbandry records stating the water was checked daily by husbandry staff. The beagles were
observed licking water off the floor grates after cleaning. The continuous watering system had previously been shut off for
an unrelated study with hounds and not turned back on when the four beagles entered the housing room. Not having
access to water can lead to serious health issues if dehydration has resulted. Potable water must be continuously
available to the dogs. Corrected before time of inspection on March 24, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the attending veterinarian and the IACUC Chairman.
n

narrative:
2.33(b)(3)  Critical
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care. 
On November 24, 2025, a technician discovered a deceased male rabbit (1M024) on a long-term surgical study. A second 
male rabbit (1M017) was found in poor condition exhibiting a body condition score of 3/9, severe malocclusion, and oral 
ulcers; it was euthanized. USDA reviewed records and found that staff failed to weigh the rabbits at the required 
frequency, did not recognize weight loss, teeth abnormalities, and did not report these problems. A lack of timely and 
accurate communication with the attending veterinarian delayed adequate treatment, resulting in illness and mortality. 
Daily observation of all animals to assess their health and wellbeing must be done. Also, a mechanism of direct and 
frequent communication is required so that timely and accurate information on problems of animal health, behavior, and 
well-being is conveyed to the attending veterinarian. Corrected before time of inspection on March 24, 2026. 
3.10(a) 
   
Watering 
Four male beagles (4267673, 4527772, 4529422, and 4661657) did not have access to water from January 27, 2025, to 
February 2, 2025, despite husbandry records stating the water was checked daily by husbandry staff. The beagles were 
observed licking water off the floor grates after cleaning. The continuous watering system had previously been shut off for 
an unrelated study with hounds and not turned back on when the four beagles entered the housing room. Not having 
access to water can lead to serious health issues if dehydration has resulted. Potable water must be continuously 
available to the dogs. Corrected before time of inspection on March 24, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the attending veterinarian and the IACUC Chairman.
